---
date: 2026/06/19
date-format: long
date-modified: last-modified
---

# BIODYNAMICS - Vegetation Co-occurrence

This project asks how much of plant co-occurrence can be attributed to shared climate responses, spatial structure, and residual species-species association signals.
It combines fossil pollen archives, modern vegetation records, climate reconstructions, site coordinates, and plant functional traits through the [VegVault](https://bit.ly/VegVault) data system.

The analysis is part of the [BIODYNAMICS](https://bit.ly/BIODYNAMICS) initiative.

::: {.callout-warning}
## Work in progress

The code, manuscript, and results are still under active development.
Methods, pipeline structure, reproducibility instructions, function documentation, and conference outputs are public, but result summaries should be treated as preliminary until the manuscript is complete.
:::

## Project Scope

The current workflow evaluates vegetation assembly along three complementary axes.

| Axis | Question | Current implementation |
|---|---|---|
| Spatial scale | Do association signals depend on geographic extent? | Paleo and modern spatial-resolution pipelines across continental, regional, and local units |
| Time | Do co-occurrence patterns change from the Last Glacial Maximum to the present? | Paleo temporal models fitted independently by time slice |
| Biological resolution | Do conclusions depend on how taxa are grouped? | Genus, family, and functional-type community matrices |

## Analysis Core

All modelled analyses use a joint species distribution modelling framework in `{sjSDM}`.
The fitted models partition community structure into an abiotic climate component, a spatial or spatiotemporal MEM component, and a residual association component.
The same core modelling stack is reused across paleo, modern, spatial, temporal, and taxonomic-resolution analyses so comparisons are traceable to a common workflow.

## Explore

- [Methods](Documentation/Website/about.qmd) describes the data streams, preprocessing, modelling core, and active pipeline design.
- [Reproducibility](Documentation/Website/installation.qmd) explains how to restore dependencies and run the current `{targets}` pipelines.
- [Outputs](Documentation/Website/outputs.qmd) links conference material, preliminary public outputs, and pipeline progress views.
- [Code Reference](Documentation/Website/Documentation/documentation.qmd) lists the generated documentation for custom R functions.
- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/OndrejMottl/BIODYNAMICS-vegetation-cooccurrence) contains the full source code and project README.
